member.summary: Lists operations that match the specified filter in the request. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `UNIMPLEMENTED`.

Optional. The maximum number of cached contents to return. The service may return fewer than this value. If unspecified, some default (under maximum) number of items will be returned. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

Optional. A page token, received from a previous `ListCachedContents` call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page. When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListCachedContents` must match the call that provided the page token.

When set to `true`, operations that are reachable are returned as normal, and those that are unreachable are returned in the [ListOperationsResponse.unreachable] field. This can only be `true` when reading across collections e.g. when `parent` is set to `"projects/example/locations/-"`. This field is not by default supported and will result in an `UNIMPLEMENTED` error if set unless explicitly documented otherwise in service or product specific documentation.

member.summary: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of `1`, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ActivityHandling
member.summary: Defines what effect activity has.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ActivityHandling.ActivityHandlingUnspecified
member.summary: If unspecified, the default behavior is `START_OF_ACTIVITY_INTERRUPTS`.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ActivityHandling.StartOfActivityInterrupts
member.summary: If true, start of activity will interrupt the model's response (also called \"barge in\"). The model's current response will be cut-off in the moment of the interruption. This is the default behavior.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ActivityHandling.NoInterruption
member.summary: The model's response will not be interrupted."

Format: `projects/{project}/secrets/{secrete}/versions/{version}` - If both `api_key_secret` and `api_key_string` are specified, this field takes precedence over `api_key_string`. - If specified, the `secretmanager.versions.access` permission should be granted to Vertex AI Extension Service Agent (https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/access-control#service-agents) on the specified resource.

member.summary: Config for Google Service Account Authentication.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthConfigGoogleServiceAccountConfig.ServiceAccount
member.summary: Optional. The service account that the extension execution service runs as. - If the service account is specified, the iam.serviceAccounts.getAccessToken permission should be granted to Vertex AI Extension Service Agent (https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/access-control#service-agents) on the specified service account. - If not specified, the Vertex AI Extension Service Agent will be used to execute the Extension.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthConfigHttpBasicAuthConfig
member.summary: Config for HTTP Basic Authentication.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthConfigHttpBasicAuthConfig.CredentialSecret
member.summary: Required. The name of the SecretManager secret version resource storing the base64 encoded credentials. Format: projects/{project}/secrets/{secrete}/versions/{version} - If specified, the secretmanager.versions.access permission should be granted to Vertex AI Extension Service Agent (https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/access-control#service-agents) on the specified resource.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthConfigOauthConfig
member.summary: Config for user oauth.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthConfigOauthConfig.AccessToken
member.summary: Access token for extension endpoint. Only used to propagate token from [[ExecuteExtensionRequest.runtime_auth_config]] at request time.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthConfigOauthConfig.ServiceAccount
member.summary: The service account used to generate access tokens for executing the Extension. - If the service account is specified, the iam.serviceAccounts.getAccessToken permission should be granted to Vertex AI Extension Service Agent (https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/access-control#service-agents) on the provided service account.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthConfigOidcConfig
member.summary: Config for user OIDC auth.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthConfigOidcConfig.IdToken
member.summary: OpenID Connect formatted ID token for extension endpoint. Only used to propagate token from [[ExecuteExtensionRequest.runtime_auth_config]] at request time.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthConfigOidcConfig.ServiceAccount
member.summary: The service account used to generate an OpenID Connect (OIDC)-compatible JWT token signed by the Google OIDC Provider (accounts.google.com) for extension endpoint (https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/create-short-lived-credentials-direct#sa-credentials-oidc). - The audience for the token will be set to the URL in the server url defined in the OpenApi spec. - If the service account is provided, the service account should grant iam.serviceAccounts.getOpenIdToken permission to Vertex AI Extension Service Agent (https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/access-control#service-agents).

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthToken
member.summary: A request to create an ephemeral authentication token.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthToken.BidiGenerateContentSetup
member.summary: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Configuration specific to BidiGenerateContent.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthToken.ExpireTime
member.summary: Optional. Input only. Immutable. An optional time after which, when using the resulting token, messages in BidiGenerateContent sessions will be rejected. (Gemini may preemptively close the session after this time.) If not set then this defaults to 30 minutes in the future. If set, this value must be less than 20 hours in the future.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthToken.FieldMask
member.summary: Optional. Input only. Immutable. If field_mask is empty, and bidi_generate_content_setup is not present, then the effective BidiGenerateContentSetup message is taken from the Live API connection. If field_mask is empty, and bidi_generate_content_setup _is_ present, then the effective BidiGenerateContentSetup message is taken entirely from bidi_generate_content_setup in this request. The setup message from the Live API connection is ignored. If field_mask is not empty, then the corresponding fields from bidi_generate_content_setup will overwrite the fields from the setup message in the Live API connection.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthToken.Name
member.summary: Output only. Identifier. The token itself.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthToken.NewSessionExpireTime
member.summary: Optional. Input only. Immutable. The time after which new Live API sessions using the token resulting from this request will be rejected. If not set this defaults to 60 seconds in the future. If set, this value must be less than 20 hours in the future.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AuthToken.Uses
member.summary: Optional. Input only. Immutable. The number of times the token can be used. If this value is zero then no limit is applied. Resuming a Live API session does not count as a use. If unspecified, the default is 1.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection
member.summary: Configures automatic detection of activity.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.Disabled
member.summary: Optional. If enabled (the default), detected voice and text input count as activity. If disabled, the client must send activity signals.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.EndOfSpeechSensitivity
member.summary: Optional. Determines how likely detected speech is ended.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.PrefixPaddingMs
member.summary: Optional. The required duration of detected speech before start-of-speech is committed. The lower this value, the more sensitive the start-of-speech detection is and shorter speech can be recognized. However, this also increases the probability of false positives.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.SilenceDurationMs
member.summary: Optional. The required duration of detected non-speech (e.g. silence) before end-of-speech is committed. The larger this value, the longer speech gaps can be without interrupting the user's activity but this will increase the model's latency.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.StartOfSpeechSensitivity
member.summary: Optional. Determines how likely speech is to be detected.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.EndOfSpeechSensitivityType.EndSensitivityUnspecified
member.summary: The default is END_SENSITIVITY_HIGH.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.EndOfSpeechSensitivityType.EndSensitivityHigh
member.summary: Automatic detection ends speech more often.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.EndOfSpeechSensitivityType.EndSensitivityLow
member.summary: Automatic detection ends speech less often.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.StartOfSpeechSensitivityType.StartSensitivityUnspecified
member.summary: The default is START_SENSITIVITY_HIGH.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.StartOfSpeechSensitivityType.StartSensitivityHigh
member.summary: Automatic detection will detect the start of speech more often.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticActivityDetection.StartOfSpeechSensitivityType.StartSensitivityLow
member.summary: Automatic detection will detect the start of speech less often.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticFunctionCallingConfig
member.summary: The configuration for automatic function calling.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticFunctionCallingConfig.Disable
member.summary: Whether to disable automatic function calling. If not set or set to False, will enable automatic function calling. If set to True, will disable automatic function calling.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticFunctionCallingConfig.IgnoreCallHistory
member.summary: If automatic function calling is enabled, whether to ignore call history to the response. If not set, SDK will set ignore_call_history to false, and will append the call history to GenerateContentResponse.automatic_function_calling_history.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutomaticFunctionCallingConfig.MaximumRemoteCalls
member.summary: If automatic function calling is enabled, maximum number of remote calls for automatic function calling. This number should be a positive integer. If not set, SDK will set maximum number of remote calls to 10.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutoraterConfig
member.summary: The configs for autorater. This is applicable to both EvaluateInstances and EvaluateDataset.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutoraterConfig.AutoraterModel
member.summary: Optional. The fully qualified name of the publisher model or tuned autorater endpoint to use. Publisher model format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/*/models/* Tuned model endpoint format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/endpoints/{endpoint}

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutoraterConfig.FlipEnabled
member.summary: Optional. Default is true. Whether to flip the candidate and baseline responses. This is only applicable to the pairwise metric. If enabled, also provide PairwiseMetricSpec.candidate_response_field_name and PairwiseMetricSpec.baseline_response_field_name. When rendering PairwiseMetricSpec.metric_prompt_template, the candidate and baseline fields will be flipped for half of the samples to reduce bias.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutoraterConfig.GenerationConfig
member.summary: Optional. Configuration options for model generation and outputs.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutoraterConfig.SamplingCount
member.summary: Optional. Number of samples for each instance in the dataset. If not specified, the default is 4. Minimum value is 1, maximum value is 32.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutoRoutingMode
member.summary: The configuration for automated routing. When automated routing is specified, the routing will be determined by the pretrained routing model and customer provided model routing preference. This data type is not supported in Gemini API.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.AutoRoutingMode.ModelRoutingPreference
member.summary: The model routing preference.

member.summary: Optional. The free-form input texts that the model will turn into an embedding. The current limit is 100 texts, over which an error will be thrown.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup
member.summary: Message to be sent in the first (and only in the first) BidiGenerateContentClientMessage. Contains configuration that will apply for the duration of the streaming RPC. Clients should wait for a BidiGenerateContentSetupComplete message before sending any additional messages.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.ContextWindowCompression
member.summary: Optional. Configures a context window compression mechanism. If included, the server will automatically reduce the size of the context when it exceeds the configured length.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.GenerationConfig
member.summary: Optional. Generation config. The following fields are not supported: - response_logprobs - response_mime_type - logprobs - response_schema - response_json_schema - stop_sequence - skip_response_cache - routing_config - audio_timestamp

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.InputAudioTranscription
member.summary: Optional. If set, enables transcription of voice input. The transcription aligns with the input audio language, if configured.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.Model
member.summary: Required. The model's resource name. This serves as an ID for the Model to use. Format: models/{model}

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.OutputAudioTranscription
member.summary: Optional. If set, enables transcription of the model's audio output. The transcription aligns with the language code specified for the output audio, if configured.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.Proactivity
member.summary: Optional. Configures the proactivity of the model. This allows the model to respond proactively to the input and to ignore irrelevant input.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.RealtimeInputConfig
member.summary: Optional. Configures the handling of realtime input.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.SessionResumption
member.summary: Optional. Configures session resumption mechanism. If included, the server will send SessionResumptionUpdate messages.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.SystemInstruction
member.summary: Optional. The user provided system instructions for the model. Note: Only text should be used in parts and content in each part will be in a separate paragraph.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BidiGenerateContentSetup.Tools
member.summary: Optional. A list of Tools the model may use to generate the next response. A Tool is a piece of code that enables the system to interact with external systems to perform an action, or set of actions, outside of knowledge and scope of the model.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BigQuerySource
member.summary: The BigQuery location for the input content.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BigQuerySource.InputUri
member.summary: Required. BigQuery URI to a table, up to 2000 characters long. Accepted forms: * BigQuery path. For example: bq://projectId.bqDatasetId.bqTableId.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BleuMetricValue
member.summary: Bleu metric value for an instance.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BleuMetricValue.Score
member.summary: Output only. Bleu score.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BleuSpec
member.summary: Spec for bleu score metric - calculates the precision of n-grams in the prediction as compared to reference - returns a score ranging between 0 to 1.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BleuSpec.UseEffectiveOrder
member.summary: Optional. Whether to use_effective_order to compute bleu score.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blob
member.summary: A content blob. A Blob contains data of a specific media type. It is used to represent images, audio, and video.
Raw media bytes. Text should not be sent as raw bytes, use the 'text' field.
Raw media bytes. Text should not be sent as raw bytes, use the 'text' field.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blob.DisplayName
member.summary: Optional. The display name of the blob. Used to provide a label or filename to distinguish blobs. This field is only returned in PromptMessage for prompt management. It is used in the Gemini calls only when server-side tools (code_execution, google_search, and url_context) are enabled.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blob.Data
member.summary: Raw bytes for media formats.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blob.MimeType
member.summary: The IANA standard MIME type of the source data. Examples: - image/png - image/jpeg If an unsupported MIME type is provided, an error will be returned. For a complete list of supported types, see [Supported file formats](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/prompting_with_media#supported_file_formats).

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blobstore2Info
member.summary: Information to read/write to blobstore2.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blobstore2Info.BlobId
member.summary: The blob id, e.g., /blobstore/prod/playground/scotty

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blobstore2Info.ReadToken
member.summary: The blob read token. Needed to read blobs that have not been replicated. Might not be available until the final call.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blobstore2Info.BlobGeneration
member.summary: The blob generation id.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blobstore2Info.UploadMetadataContainer
member.summary: Metadata passed from Blobstore -> Scotty for a new GCS upload. This is a signed, serialized blobstore2.BlobMetadataContainer proto which must never be consumed outside of Bigstore, and is not applicable to non-GCS media uploads.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Blobstore2Info.DownloadReadHandle
member.summary: Read handle passed from Bigstore -> Scotty for a GCS download. This is a signed, serialized blobstore2.ReadHandle proto which must never be set outside of Bigstore, and is not applicable to non-GCS media downloads.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BlurBaselineConfig
member.summary: Config for blur baseline. When enabled, a linear path from the maximally blurred image to the input image is created. Using a blurred baseline instead of zero (black image) is motivated by the BlurIG approach explained here: https://arxiv.org/abs/2004.03383

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.BlurBaselineConfig.MaxBlurSigma
member.summary: The standard deviation of the blur kernel for the blurred baseline. The same blurring parameter is used for both the height and the width dimension. If not set, the method defaults to the zero (i.e. black for images) baseline.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent
member.summary: A resource used in LLM queries for users to explicitly specify what to cache and how to cache.
Content that has been preprocessed and can be used in subsequent request to GenerativeService. Cached content can be only used with model it was created for.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.EncryptionSpec
member.summary: Input only. Immutable. Customer-managed encryption key spec for a CachedContent. If set, this CachedContent and all its sub-resources will be secured by this key.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.Model
member.summary: Required. Immutable. The name of the `Model` to use for cached content Format: `models/{model}`

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.Name
member.summary: Optional. Identifier. The resource name referring to the cached content. Format: `cachedContents/{id}`

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.Expiration
member.summary: Specifies when this resource will expire.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.Ttl
member.summary: Input only. New TTL for this resource, input only.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.Contents
member.summary: Optional. Input only. Immutable. The content to cache.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.CreateTime
member.summary: Output only. Creation time of the cache entry.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.DisplayName
member.summary: Optional. Immutable. The user-generated meaningful display name of the cached content. Maximum 128 Unicode characters.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.ExpireTime
member.summary: Timestamp in UTC of when this resource is considered expired. This is *always* provided on output, regardless of what was sent on input.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.SystemInstruction
member.summary: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Developer set system instruction. Currently text only.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.ToolConfig
member.summary: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Tool config. This config is shared for all tools.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.Tools
member.summary: Optional. Input only. Immutable. A list of Tools the model may use to generate the next response

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.UpdateTime
member.summary: Output only. When the cache entry was last updated in UTC time.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContent.UsageMetadata
member.summary: Output only. Metadata on the usage of the cached content.

member.summary: Metadata on the usage of the cached content.
Metadata on the usage of the cached content.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContentUsageMetadata.AudioDurationSeconds
member.summary: Duration of audio in seconds.

member.summary: Number of images.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CachedContentUsageMetadata.VideoDurationSeconds

member.summary: Output only. Generated content returned from the model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.UrlRetrievalMetadata
member.summary: Output only. Metadata related to url context retrieval tool.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.AvgLogprobs
member.summary: Output only. Average log probability score of the candidate.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.CitationMetadata
member.summary: Output only. Citation information for model-generated candidate. This field may be populated with recitation information for any text included in the content. These are passages that are "recited" from copyrighted material in the foundational LLM's training data.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.FinishMessage
member.summary: Optional. Output only. Details the reason why the model stopped generating tokens. This is populated only when finish_reason is set.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.FinishReason
member.summary: Optional. Output only. The reason why the model stopped generating tokens. If empty, the model has not stopped generating tokens.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.GroundingAttributions
member.summary: Output only. Attribution information for sources that contributed to a grounded answer. This field is populated for GenerateAnswer calls.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.GroundingMetadata
member.summary: Output only. Grounding metadata for the candidate. This field is populated for GenerateContent calls.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.Index
member.summary: Output only. Index of the candidate in the list of response candidates.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.LogprobsResult
member.summary: Output only. Log-likelihood scores for the response tokens and top tokens

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.SafetyRatings
member.summary: List of ratings for the safety of a response candidate. There is at most one rating per category.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.TokenCount
member.summary: Output only. Token count for this candidate.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Candidate.UrlContextMetadata
member.summary: Output only. Metadata related to url context retrieval tool.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest
member.summary: Request for chat completions.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.Model
member.summary: Required. The name of the `Model` to use for generating the completion. The model name will prefixed by \"models/\" if no slash appears in it.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.Messages
member.summary: Required. The chat history to use for generating the completion. Supports single and multi-turn queries. Note: This is a polymorphic field, it is deserialized to a InternalChatMessage.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.MaxCompletionTokens
member.summary: Optional. The maximum number of tokens to include in a response candidate. Must be a positive integer.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.MaxTokens
member.summary: Optional. The maximum number of tokens to include in a response candidate. Must be a positive integer. This field is deprecated by the SDK.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.N
member.summary: Optional. Amount of candidate completions to generate. Must be a positive integer. Defaults to 1 if not set.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.ResponseFormat
member.summary: Optional. Defines the format of the response. If not set, the response will be formatted as text.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.Stop
member.summary: Optional. The set of character sequences that will stop output generation. Note: This is a polymorphic field. It is meant to contain a string or repeated strings.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.Stream
member.summary: Optional. Whether to stream the response or return a single response. If true, the \"object\" field in the response will be \"chat.completion.chunk\". Otherwise it will be \"chat.completion\".

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.StreamOptions
member.summary: Optional. Options for streaming requests.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.Temperature
member.summary: Optional. Controls the randomness of the output.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.TopP
member.summary: Optional. The maximum cumulative probability of tokens to consider when sampling.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.ToolChoice
member.summary: Optional. Controls whether the model should use a tool or not, and which tool to use. Can be either: - The string \"none\", to disable tools. - The string \"auto\", to let the model decide. - The string \"required\", to force the model to use a tool. - A function name descriptor object, specifying the tool to use. The last option follows the following schema: { \"type\": \"function\", \"function\": {\"name\" : \"the_function_name\"} }

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.Tools
member.summary: Optional. The set of tools the model can generate calls for. Each tool declares its signature.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.Audio
member.summary: Optional. Options for audio generation.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.Modalities
member.summary: Optional. Modalities for the request.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.ParallelToolCalls
member.summary: Optional. Whether to call tools in parallel.
member.remarks: Included here for compatibility with the SDK, but only false is supported.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.PresencePenalty
member.summary: Optional. Penalizes new tokens based on previous appearances. Valid ranges are [-2, 2]. Default is 0.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatCompletionsRequest.User
member.summary: Optional. The user name used for tracking the request. Not used, only for compatibility with the SDK.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatFunction
member.summary: A function that the model can generate calls for.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatFunction.Name
member.summary: Required. The name of the function.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatFunction.Description
member.summary: Optional. A description of the function.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatFunction.Strict
member.summary: Optional. Whether the schema validation is strict. If true, the model will fail if the schema is not valid. NOTE: This parameter is currently ignored.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ChatFunction.Parameters
member.summary: Optional. The parameters of the function.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Chunk.Name
member.summary: Immutable. Identifier. The resource name. The ID (name excluding the "corpora/*/documents/*/chunks/" prefix) can contain up to 40 characters that are lowercase alphanumeric or dashes (-). The ID cannot start or end with a dash. If the name is empty on create, a random 12-character unique ID will be generated. Example:

member.summary: For Scotty Uploads: Scotty-provided hashes for uploads For Scotty Downloads: (WARNING: DO NOT USE WITHOUT PERMISSION FROM THE SCOTTY TEAM.) A Hash provided by the agent to be used to verify the data being downloaded. Currently only supported for inline payloads. Further, only crc32c_hash is currently supported.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CompositeMedia.BlobRef
member.summary: Blobstore v1 reference, set if reference_type is BLOBSTORE_REF This should be the byte representation of a blobstore.BlobRef. Since Blobstore is deprecating v1, use blobstore2_info instead. For now, any v2 blob will also be represented in this field as v1 BlobRef.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CompositeMedia.Length
member.summary: Size of the data, in bytes

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CompositeMedia.ObjectId
member.summary: Reference to a TI Blob, set if reference_type is BIGSTORE_REF.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CompositeMedia.CosmoBinaryReference
member.summary: A binary data reference for a media download. Serves as a technology-agnostic binary reference in some Google infrastructure. This value is a serialized storage_cosmo.BinaryReference proto. Storing it as bytes is a hack to get around the fact that the cosmo proto (as well as others it includes) doesn't support JavaScript. This prevents us from including the actual type of this field.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CompositeMedia.Blobstore2Info
member.summary: Blobstore v2 info, set if reference_type is BLOBSTORE_REF and it refers to a v2 blob.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ContentTypeInfo
member.summary: Detailed Content-Type information from Scotty. The Content-Type of the media will typically be filled in by the header or Scotty's best_guess, but this extended information provides the backend with more information so that it can make a better decision if needed. This is only used on media upload requests from Scotty.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ContentTypeInfo.FromBytes
member.summary: The content type of the file derived by looking at specific bytes (i.e. \"magic bytes\") of the actual file.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ContentTypeInfo.FromUrlPath
member.summary: The content type of the file derived from the file extension of the URL path. The URL path is assumed to represent a file name (which is typically only true for agents that are providing a REST API).

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ContentTypeInfo.FromHeader
member.summary: The content type of the file as specified in the request headers, multipart headers, or RUPIO start request.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ContentTypeInfo.FromFileName
member.summary: The content type of the file derived from the file extension of the original file name used by the client.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ContentTypeInfo.BestGuess
member.summary: Scotty's best guess of what the content type of the file is.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ContextWindowCompressionConfig
member.summary: Enables context window compression — a mechanism for managing the model's context window so that it does not exceed a given length.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ContextWindowCompressionConfig.SlidingWindow
member.summary: A sliding-window mechanism.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ContextWindowCompressionConfig.TriggerTokens
member.summary: The number of tokens (before running a turn) required to trigger a context window compression. This can be used to balance quality against latency as shorter context windows may result in faster model responses. However, any compression operation will cause a temporary latency increase, so they should not be triggered frequently. If not set, the default is 80% of the model's context window limit. This leaves 20% for the next user request/model response.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Corpus
member.summary: A is a collection of s. A project can create up to 10 corpora.
A Corpus is a collection of Documents. A project can create up to 10 corpora.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Corpus.CreateTime
member.summary: Output only. The Timestamp of when the Corpus was created.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Corpus.DisplayName
member.summary: Optional. The human-readable display name for the Corpus. The display name must be no more than 512 characters in length, including spaces. Example: "Docs on Semantic Retriever"

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Corpus.Name
member.summary: Output only. Immutable. Identifier. The Corpus resource name. The ID (name excluding the "corpora/" prefix) can contain up to 40 characters that are lowercase alphanumeric or dashes (-). The ID cannot start or end with a dash. If the name is empty on create, a unique name will be derived from display_name along with a 12 character random suffix. Example: corpora/my-awesome-corpora-123a456b789c

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Corpus.UpdateTime
member.summary: Output only. The Timestamp of when the Corpus was last updated.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CorpusQueryRequest
member.summary: Request for querying a `Corpus`.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CorpusQueryRequest.Query
member.summary: Required. Query string to perform semantic search.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CorpusQueryRequest.MetadataFilters
member.summary: Optional. Filter for `Chunk` and `Document` metadata. Each `MetadataFilter` object should correspond to a unique key. Multiple `MetadataFilter` objects are joined by logical \"AND\"s. Example query at document level: (year >= 2020 OR year < 2010) AND (genre = drama OR genre = action) `MetadataFilter` object list: metadata_filters = [ {key = \"document.custom_metadata.year\" conditions = [{int_value = 2020, operation = GREATER_EQUAL}, {int_value = 2010, operation = LESS}]}, {key = \"document.custom_metadata.year\" conditions = [{int_value = 2020, operation = GREATER_EQUAL}, {int_value = 2010, operation = LESS}]}, {key = \"document.custom_metadata.genre\" conditions = [{string_value = \"drama\", operation = EQUAL}, {string_value = \"action\", operation = EQUAL}]}] Example query at chunk level for a numeric range of values: (year > 2015 AND year <= 2020) `MetadataFilter` object list: metadata_filters = [ {key = \"chunk.custom_metadata.year\" conditions = [{int_value = 2015, operation = GREATER}]}, {key = \"chunk.custom_metadata.year\" conditions = [{int_value = 2020, operation = LESS_EQUAL}]}] Note: \"AND\"s for the same key are only supported for numeric values. String values only support \"OR\"s for the same key.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CorpusQueryRequest.ResultsCount
member.summary: Optional. The maximum number of `Chunk`s to return. The service may return fewer `Chunk`s. If unspecified, at most 10 `Chunk`s will be returned. The maximum specified result count is 100.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CorpusQueryResponse
member.summary: Response from `QueryCorpus` containing a list of relevant chunks.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CorpusQueryResponse.RelevantChunks
member.summary: The relevant chunks.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensRequest
member.summary: Request message for PredictionService.CountTokens.
Request for counting tokens.
Counts the number of tokens in the prompt sent to a model. Models may tokenize text differently, so each model may return a different token_count.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensRequest.GenerationConfig
member.summary: Optional. Generation config that the model will use to generate the response.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensRequest.Instances
member.summary: Optional. The instances that are the input to token counting call. Schema is identical to the prediction schema of the underlying model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensRequest.Model
member.summary: Optional. The name of the publisher model requested to serve the prediction. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/*/models/*

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensRequest.SystemInstruction
member.summary: Optional. The user provided system instructions for the model. Note: only text should be used in parts and content in each part will be in a separate paragraph.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensRequest.Tools
member.summary: Optional. A list of Tools the model may use to generate the next response. A Tool is a piece of code that enables the system to interact with external systems to perform an action, or set of actions, outside of knowledge and scope of the model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensRequest.Config
member.summary: Configuration for counting tokens.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensRequest.Contents
member.summary: Optional. The input given to the model as a prompt. This field is ignored when generate_content_request is set.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensRequest.GenerateContentRequest
member.summary: Optional. The overall input given to the Model. This includes the prompt as well as other model steering information like [system instructions](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/system-instructions), and/or function declarations for [function calling](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/function-calling). Models/Contents and generate_content_requests are mutually exclusive. You can either send Model + Contents or a generate_content_request, but never both.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensResponse
member.summary: Response message for PredictionService.CountTokens.
A response from `CountTokens`. It returns the model's `token_count` for the `prompt`.
A response from CountTokens. It returns the model's token_count for the prompt.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensResponse.TotalBillableCharacters
member.summary: The total number of billable characters counted across all instances from the request.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensResponse.TotalTokens
member.summary: The number of tokens that the tokenizes the into. Always non-negative.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensResponse.TokenCount
member.summary: The total number of tokens counted across all instances from the request.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensResponse.CachedContentTokenCount
member.summary: Number of tokens in the cached part of the prompt (the cached content).

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensResponse.CacheTokensDetails

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CountTokensResponse.PromptTokensDetails

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CustomCodeExecutionResult
member.summary: Result for custom code execution metric.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CustomCodeExecutionResult.Score
member.summary: Output only. Custom code execution score.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CustomCodeExecutionSpec
member.summary: Specificies a metric that is populated by evaluating user-defined Python code.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CustomCodeExecutionSpec.EvaluationFunction
member.summary: Required. Python function. Expected user to define the following function, e.g.: def evaluate(instance: dict[str, Any]) -> float: Please include this function signature in the code snippet. Instance is the evaluation instance, any fields populated in the instance are available to the function as instance[field_name]. Example: Example input: `` instance= EvaluationInstance( response=EvaluationInstance.InstanceData(text="The answer is 4."), reference=EvaluationInstance.InstanceData(text="4") ) ` Example converted input: ` { 'response': {'text': 'The answer is 4.'}, 'reference': {'text': '4'} } ` Example python function: ` def evaluate(instance: dict[str, Any]) -> float: if instance'response' == instance'reference': return 1.0 return 0.0 `` CustomCodeExecutionSpec is also supported in Batch Evaluation (EvalDataset RPC) and Tuning Evaluation. Each line in the input jsonl file will be converted to dict[str, Any] and passed to the evaluation function.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CustomLongRunningOperation.Done
member.summary: If the value is false, it means the operation is still in progress. If true, the operation is completed, and either error or response is available.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CustomLongRunningOperation.Error
member.summary: The error result of the operation in case of failure or cancellation.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CustomLongRunningOperation.Metadata
member.summary: Service-specific metadata associated with the operation. It typically contains progress information and common metadata such as create time. Some services might not provide such metadata. Any method that returns a long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CustomLongRunningOperation.Name
member.summary: The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the name should be a resource name ending with operations/{unique_id}.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.CustomLongRunningOperation.Response
member.summary: The normal, successful response of the operation. If the original method returns no data on success, such as Delete, the response is google.protobuf.Empty. If the original method is standard Get/Create/Update, the response should be the resource. For other methods, the response should have the type XxxResponse, where Xxx is the original method name. For example, if the original method name is TakeSnapshot(), the inferred response type is TakeSnapshotResponse.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Dataset.Etag
member.summary: Used to perform consistent read-modify-write updates. If not set, a blind "overwrite" update happens.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Dataset.Labels
member.summary: The labels with user-defined metadata to organize your Datasets. Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. No more than 64 user labels can be associated with one Dataset (System labels are excluded). See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels. System reserved label keys are prefixed with "aiplatform.googleapis.com/" and are immutable. Following system labels exist for each Dataset: * "aiplatform.googleapis.com/dataset_metadata_schema": output only, its value is the metadata_schema's title.

member.summary: The object version of the object the checksums are being returned for.

member.summary: The total size of the server object.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.DiffChecksumsResponse.ChunkSizeBytes
member.summary: The chunk size of checksums. Must be a multiple of 256KB.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.DiffChecksumsResponse.ObjectLocation
member.summary: If set, calculate the checksums based on the contents and return them to the caller.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.DiffChecksumsResponse.ChecksumsLocation
member.summary: Exactly one of these fields must be populated. If checksums_location is filled, the server will return the corresponding contents to the user. If object_location is filled, the server will calculate the checksums based on the content there and return that to the user. For details on the format of the checksums, see http://go/scotty-diff-protocol.

member.summary: The location of the checksums for the new object. Agents must clone the object located here, as the upload server will delete the contents once a response is received. For details on the format of the checksums, see http://go/scotty-diff-protocol.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.DiffUploadResponse.ObjectVersion

member.summary: Output only. The Timestamp of when the Document was created.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Document.CustomMetadata
member.summary: Optional. User provided custom metadata stored as key-value pairs used for querying. A Document can have a maximum of 20 CustomMetadata.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Document.DisplayName
member.summary: Optional. The human-readable display name for the Document. The display name must be no more than 512 characters in length, including spaces. Example: "Semantic Retriever Documentation"

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Document.MimeType
member.summary: Output only. The mime type of the Document.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Document.Name
member.summary: Immutable. Identifier. The Document resource name. The ID (name excluding the "fileSearchStores/*/documents/" prefix) can contain up to 40 characters that are lowercase alphanumeric or dashes (-). The ID cannot start or end with a dash. If the name is empty on create, a unique name will be derived from display_name along with a 12 character random suffix. Example: fileSearchStores/{file_search_store_id}/documents/my-awesome-doc-123a456b789c

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Document.SizeBytes
member.summary: Output only. The size of raw bytes ingested into the Document.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Document.StateType.StatePending
member.summary: Some `Chunks` of the `Document` are being processed (embedding and vector storage).

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Document.StateType.StateActive

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.DownloadParameters.AllowGzipCompression
member.summary: A boolean to be returned in the response to Scotty. Allows/disallows gzip encoding of the payload content when the server thinks it's advantageous (hence, does not guarantee compression) which allows Scotty to GZip the response to the client.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.DownloadParameters.IgnoreRange
member.summary: Determining whether or not Apiary should skip the inclusion of any Content-Range header on its response to Scotty.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Duration
member.summary: A Duration represents a signed, fixed-length span of time represented as a count of seconds and fractions of seconds at nanosecond resolution.
member.remarks: It is independent of any calendar and concepts like "day" or "month". It is related to Timestamp in that the difference between two Timestamp values is a Duration and it can be added or subtracted from a Timestamp. Range is approximately +-10,000 years.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Duration.Seconds
member.summary: Seconds of a duration.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.EditImageConfig.ProductPosition
member.summary: Optional. Defines whether the detected product should stay fixed or be repositioned. If you set this field, you must also set "editMode": "product-image".
member.remarks: Values: reposition - Lets the model move the location of the detected product or object. (default value) fixed - The model maintains the original positioning of the detected product or object If the input image is not square, the model defaults to reposition.

member.summary: Reduced dimension for the output embedding. If set, excessive values in the output embedding are truncated from the end. Supported by newer models since 2024 only. You cannot set this value if using the earlier model (models/embedding-001).

member.summary: Optional. Whether to silently truncate the input content if it's longer than the maximum sequence length.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.EmbedContentRequest.TaskType
member.summary: Optional. Optional task type for which the embeddings will be used. Not supported on earlier models (models/embedding-001).

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.EmbedContentRequest.Title
member.summary: Optional. An optional title for the text. Only applicable when TaskType is RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT. Note: Specifying a title for RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT provides better quality embeddings for retrieval.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.EnterpriseWebSearch.TimeRangeFilter
member.summary: Optional. Filter search results to a specific time range. If customers set a start time, they must set an end time (and vice versa). This field is not supported in Vertex AI.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.EnvVar
member.summary: Represents an environment variable present in a Container or Python Module.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.EnvVar.Name
member.summary: Required. Name of the environment variable. Must be a valid C identifier.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.EnvVar.Value
member.summary: Required. Variables that reference a $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the previous defined environment variables in the container and any service environment variables. If a variable cannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. The $(VAR_NAME) syntax can be escaped with a double $$, ie: $$(VAR_NAME). Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless of whether the variable exists or not.

member.summary: Required. The code to be executed.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationMetadata
member.summary: Metadata describing the Model's input and output for explanation.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationMetadata.FeatureAttributionsSchemaUri
member.summary: Points to a YAML file stored on Google Cloud Storage describing the format of the feature attributions. The schema is defined as an OpenAPI 3.0.2 [Schema Object](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/main/versions/3.0.2.md#schemaObject). AutoML tabular Models always have this field populated by Vertex AI. Note: The URI given on output may be different, including the URI scheme, than the one given on input. The output URI will point to a location where the user only has a read access.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationMetadata.Inputs
member.summary: Required. Map from feature names to feature input metadata. Keys are the name of the features. Values are the specification of the feature. An empty InputMetadata is valid. It describes a text feature which has the name specified as the key in ExplanationMetadata.inputs. The baseline of the empty feature is chosen by Vertex AI. For Vertex AI-provided Tensorflow images, the key can be any friendly name of the feature. Once specified, featureAttributions are keyed by this key (if not grouped with another feature). For custom images, the key must match with the key in instance.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationMetadata.LatentSpaceSource
member.summary: Name of the source to generate embeddings for example based explanations.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationMetadata.Outputs
member.summary: Required. Map from output names to output metadata. For Vertex AI-provided Tensorflow images, keys can be any user defined string that consists of any UTF-8 characters. For custom images, keys are the name of the output field in the prediction to be explained. Currently only one key is allowed.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationParameters
member.summary: Parameters to configure explaining for Model's predictions.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationParameters.Examples
member.summary: Example-based explanations that returns the nearest neighbors from the provided dataset.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationParameters.IntegratedGradientsAttribution
member.summary: An attribution method that computes Aumann-Shapley values taking advantage of the model's fully differentiable structure. Refer to this paper for more details: https://arxiv.org/abs/1703.01365

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationParameters.OutputIndices
member.summary: If populated, only returns attributions that have output_index contained in output_indices. It must be an ndarray of integers, with the same shape of the output it's explaining. If not populated, returns attributions for top_k indices of outputs. If neither top_k nor output_indices is populated, returns the argmax index of the outputs. Only applicable to Models that predict multiple outputs (e,g, multi-class Models that predict multiple classes).

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationParameters.SampledShapleyAttribution
member.summary: An attribution method that approximates Shapley values for features that contribute to the label being predicted. A sampling strategy is used to approximate the value rather than considering all subsets of features. Refer to this paper for model details: https://arxiv.org/abs/1306.4265.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationParameters.TopK
member.summary: If populated, returns attributions for top K indices of outputs (defaults to 1). Only applies to Models that predicts more than one outputs (e,g, multi-class Models). When set to -1, returns explanations for all outputs.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationParameters.XraiAttribution
member.summary: An attribution method that redistributes Integrated Gradients attribution to segmented regions, taking advantage of the model's fully differentiable structure. Refer to this paper for more details: https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.02825 XRAI currently performs better on natural images, like a picture of a house or an animal. If the images are taken in artificial environments, like a lab or manufacturing line, or from diagnostic equipment, like x-rays or quality-control cameras, use Integrated Gradients instead.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationSpec
member.summary: Specification of Model explanation.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationSpec.Metadata
member.summary: Optional. Metadata describing the Model's input and output for explanation.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ExplanationSpec.Parameters
member.summary: Required. Parameters that configure explaining of the Model's predictions.

member.summary: Noise sigma by features. Noise sigma represents the standard deviation of the gaussian kernel that will be used to add noise to interpolated inputs prior to computing gradients.

member.summary: Noise sigma for a single feature.

member.summary: The name of the input feature for which noise sigma is provided. The features are defined in explanation metadata inputs.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FeatureNoiseSigmaNoiseSigmaForFeature.Sigma
member.summary: This represents the standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel that will be used to add noise to the feature prior to computing gradients. Similar to noise_sigma but represents the noise added to the current feature. Defaults to 0.1.

Example: fileSearchStores/my-awesome-file-search-store-123a456b789c If display_name is not provided, the name will be randomly generated.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCall
member.summary: A predicted [FunctionCall] returned from the model that contains a string representing the [FunctionDeclaration.name] and a structured JSON object containing the parameters and their values.
A predicted returned from the model that contains a string representing the with the arguments and their values.
A predicted FunctionCall returned from the model that contains a string representing the FunctionDeclaration.name with the arguments and their values.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCall.PartialArgs
member.summary: Optional. The partial argument value of the function call. If provided, represents the arguments/fields that are streamed incrementally.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCall.WillContinue
member.summary: Optional. Whether this is the last part of the FunctionCall. If true, another partial message for the current FunctionCall is expected to follow.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCall.Args
member.summary: Optional. The function parameters and values in JSON object format.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCall.Id
member.summary: Optional. Unique identifier of the function call. If populated, the client to execute the function_call and return the response with the matching id.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCall.Name
member.summary: Required. The name of the function to call. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCallingConfig
member.summary: Function calling config.
Configuration for specifying function calling behavior.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCallingConfig.StreamFunctionCallArguments
member.summary: Optional. When set to true, arguments of a single function call will be streamed out in multiple parts/contents/responses. Partial parameter results will be returned in the [FunctionCall.partial_args] field.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCallingConfig.AllowedFunctionNames
member.summary: Optional. A set of function names that, when provided, limits the functions the model will call. This should only be set when the Mode is ANY or VALIDATED. Function names should match [FunctionDeclaration.name]. When set, model will predict a function call from only allowed function names.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCallingConfig.Mode
member.summary: Optional. Specifies the mode in which function calling should execute. If unspecified, the default value will be set to AUTO.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.ModeUnspecified
member.summary: Unspecified function calling mode. This value should not be used.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.Auto
member.summary: Default model behavior, model decides to predict either a function call or a natural language response.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.Any
member.summary: Model is constrained to always predicting a function call only. If "allowed_function_names" are set, the predicted function call will be limited to any one of "allowed_function_names", else the predicted function call will be any one of the provided "function_declarations".

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.None
member.summary: Model will not predict any function call. Model behavior is same as when not passing any function declarations.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionCallingConfigMode.Validated
member.summary: Model decides to predict either a function call or a natural language response, but will validate function calls with constrained decoding. If "allowed_function_names" are set, the predicted function call will be limited to any one of "allowed_function_names", else the predicted function call will be any one of the provided "function_declarations".

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration
member.summary: Structured representation of a function declaration as defined by the [OpenAPI 3.0 specification](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3). Included in this declaration are the function name, description, parameters and response type. This FunctionDeclaration is a representation of a block of code that can be used as a Tool by the model and executed by the client.

Structured representation of a function declaration as defined by the OpenAPI 3.03 specification. Included in this declaration are the function name and parameters. This FunctionDeclaration is a representation of a block of code that can be used as a Tool by the model and executed by the client.

Structured representation of a function declaration as defined by the [OpenAPI 3.03 specification](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3). Included in this declaration are the function name and parameters. This FunctionDeclaration is a representation of a block of code that can be used as a Tool by the model and executed by the client.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.Behavior
member.summary: Optional. Specifies the function Behavior. Currently only supported by the BidiGenerateContent method.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.Description
member.summary: Required. A brief description of the function.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.Name
member.summary: Required. The name of the function. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores, colons, dots, and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.Parameters
member.summary: Optional. Describes the parameters to this function. Reflects the Open API 3.03 Parameter Object string Key: the name of the parameter. Parameter names are case sensitive. Schema Value: the Schema defining the type used for the parameter.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.ParametersJsonSchema
member.summary: Optional. Describes the parameters to the function in JSON Schema format. The schema must describe an object where the properties are the parameters to the function. For example: `` { "type": "object", "properties": { "name": { "type": "string" }, "age": { "type": "integer" } }, "additionalProperties": false, "required": ["name", "age"], "propertyOrdering": ["name", "age"] } ` This field is mutually exclusive with parameters`.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.Response
member.summary: Optional. Describes the output from this function in JSON Schema format. Reflects the Open API 3.03 Response Object. The Schema defines the type used for the response value of the function.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.ResponseJsonSchema
member.summary: Optional. Describes the output from this function in JSON Schema format. The value specified by the schema is the response value of the function. This field is mutually exclusive with response.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.BehaviorType.Unspecified
member.summary: This value is unused.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.BehaviorType.Blocking
member.summary: If set, the system will wait to receive the function response before continuing the conversation.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionDeclaration.BehaviorType.NonBlocking
member.summary: If set, the system will not wait to receive the function response. Instead, it will attempt to handle function responses as they become available while maintaining the conversation between the user and the model.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse
member.summary: The result output from a [FunctionCall] that contains a string representing the [FunctionDeclaration.name] and a structured JSON object containing any output from the function is used as context to the model. This should contain the result of a [FunctionCall] made based on model prediction.

The result output from a that contains a string representing the and a structured JSON object containing any output from the function is used as context to the model. This should contain the result of a made based on model prediction.

The result output from a FunctionCall that contains a string representing the FunctionDeclaration.name and a structured JSON object containing any output from the function is used as context to the model. This should contain the result of aFunctionCall made based on model prediction.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.Id
member.summary: Optional. The identifier of the function call this response is for. Populated by the client to match the corresponding function call id.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.Name
member.summary: Required. The name of the function to call. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.Parts
member.summary: Optional. Ordered Parts that constitute a function response. Parts may have different IANA MIME types.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.Response
member.summary: Required. The function response in JSON object format. Callers can use any keys of their choice that fit the function's syntax to return the function output, e.g. "output", "result", etc. In particular, if the function call failed to execute, the response can have an "error" key to return error details to the model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.Scheduling
member.summary: Optional. Specifies how the response should be scheduled in the conversation. Only applicable to NON_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. Defaults to WHEN_IDLE.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.WillContinue
member.summary: Optional. Signals that function call continues, and more responses will be returned, turning the function call into a generator. Is only applicable to NON_BLOCKING function calls, is ignored otherwise. If set to false, future responses will not be considered. It is allowed to return empty response with will_continue=False to signal that the function call is finished. This may still trigger the model generation. To avoid triggering the generation and finish the function call, additionally set scheduling to SILENT.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.SchedulingType.SchedulingUnspecified
member.summary: This value is unused.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.SchedulingType.Silent
member.summary: Only add the result to the conversation context, do not interrupt or trigger generation.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.SchedulingType.WhenIdle
member.summary: Add the result to the conversation context, and prompt to generate output without interrupting ongoing generation.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponse.SchedulingType.Interrupt
member.summary: Add the result to the conversation context, interrupt ongoing generation and prompt to generate output.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.FunctionResponseBlob
member.summary: Raw media bytes for function response. Text should not be sent as raw bytes, use the 'text' field.
Raw media bytes for function response. Text should not be sent as raw bytes, use the 'FunctionResponse.response' field.

For a complete list of supported types, see [Supported file formats](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/prompting_with_media#supported_file_formats).

member.summary: A datatype containing media that is part of a FunctionResponse message. A FunctionResponsePart consists of data which has an associated datatype. member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerRequest
member.summary: Request to generate a grounded answer from the model.
Request to generate a grounded answer from the Model.

member.name: M:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerRequest.#ctor
member.summary: Default constructor.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerRequest.AnswerStyle
member.summary: Required. Style in which answers should be returned.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerRequest.Contents
member.summary: Required. The content of the current conversation with the Model. For single-turn queries, this is a single question to answer. For multi-turn queries, this is a repeated field that contains conversation history and the last Content in the list containing the question. Note: GenerateAnswer only supports queries in English.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerRequest.InlinePassages
member.summary: Passages provided inline with the request.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerRequest.SafetySettings
member.summary: Optional. A list of unique SafetySetting instances for blocking unsafe content. This will be enforced on the GenerateAnswerRequest.contents and GenerateAnswerResponse.candidate. There should not be more than one setting for each SafetyCategory type. The API will block any contents and responses that fail to meet the thresholds set by these settings. This list overrides the default settings for each SafetyCategory specified in the safety_settings. If there is no SafetySetting for a given SafetyCategory provided in the list, the API will use the default safety setting for that category. Harm categories HARM_CATEGORY_HATE_SPEECH, HARM_CATEGORY_SEXUALLY_EXPLICIT, HARM_CATEGORY_DANGEROUS_CONTENT, HARM_CATEGORY_HARASSMENT are supported. Refer to the [guide](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/safety-settings) for detailed information on available safety settings. Also refer to the [Safety guidance](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/safety-guidance) to learn how to incorporate safety considerations in your AI applications.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerRequest.SemanticRetriever
member.summary: Content retrieved from resources created via the Semantic Retriever API.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerRequest.Temperature
member.summary: Optional. Controls the randomness of the output. Values can range from [0.0,1.0], inclusive. A value closer to 1.0 will produce responses that are more varied and creative, while a value closer to 0.0 will typically result in more straightforward responses from the model. A low temperature (~0.2) is usually recommended for Attributed-Question-Answering use cases.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerResponse
member.summary: Response from the model for a grounded answer.
Response from the model for a grounded answer.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerResponse.Text
member.summary: Responded text information of first candidate.

member.name: M:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerResponse.ToString
member.summary: A convenience overload to easily access the responded text.
member.returns: The responded text information of first candidate.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerResponse.Answer
member.summary: Candidate answer from the model. Note: The model *always* attempts to provide a grounded answer, even when the answer is unlikely to be answerable from the given passages. In that case, a low-quality or ungrounded answer may be provided, along with a low answerable_probability.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerResponse.AnswerableProbability
member.summary: Output only. The model's estimate of the probability that its answer is correct and grounded in the input passages. A low answerable_probability indicates that the answer might not be grounded in the sources. When answerable_probability is low, you may want to: * Display a message to the effect of "We couldn’t answer that question" to the user. * Fall back to a general-purpose LLM that answers the question from world knowledge. The threshold and nature of such fallbacks will depend on individual use cases. 0.5 is a good starting threshold.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateAnswerResponse.InputFeedback
member.summary: Output only. Feedback related to the input data used to answer the question, as opposed to the model-generated response to the question. The input data can be one or more of the following: - Question specified by the last entry in GenerateAnswerRequest.content - Conversation history specified by the other entries in GenerateAnswerRequest.content - Grounding sources (GenerateAnswerRequest.semantic_retriever or GenerateAnswerRequest.inline_passages)

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateContentRequest.Contents
member.summary: Required. The content of the current conversation with the model. For single-turn queries, this is a single instance. For multi-turn queries like [chat](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/text-generation#chat), this is a repeated field that contains the conversation history and the latest request.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateContentRequest.GenerationConfig
member.summary: Optional. Configuration options for model generation and outputs.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateContentRequest.Model
member.summary: Required. The name of the Model to use for generating the completion. Format: models/{model}.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateContentRequest.SafetySettings
member.summary: Optional. A list of unique SafetySetting instances for blocking unsafe content. This will be enforced on the GenerateContentRequest.contents and GenerateContentResponse.candidates. There should not be more than one setting for each SafetyCategory type. The API will block any contents and responses that fail to meet the thresholds set by these settings. This list overrides the default settings for each SafetyCategory specified in the safety_settings. If there is no SafetySetting for a given SafetyCategory provided in the list, the API will use the default safety setting for that category. Harm categories HARM_CATEGORY_HATE_SPEECH, HARM_CATEGORY_SEXUALLY_EXPLICIT, HARM_CATEGORY_DANGEROUS_CONTENT, HARM_CATEGORY_HARASSMENT, HARM_CATEGORY_CIVIC_INTEGRITY are supported. Refer to the [guide](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/safety-settings) for detailed information on available safety settings. Also refer to the [Safety guidance](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/safety-guidance) to learn how to incorporate safety considerations in your AI applications.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateContentRequest.SystemInstruction
member.summary: Optional. Developer set [system instruction(s)](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/system-instructions). Currently, text only.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateContentRequest.ToolConfig
member.summary: Optional. Tool configuration for any Tool specified in the request. Refer to the [Function calling guide](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/function-calling#function_calling_mode) for a usage example.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateContentRequest.Tools
member.summary: Optional. A list of Tools the Model may use to generate the next response. A Tool is a piece of code that enables the system to interact with external systems to perform an action, or set of actions, outside of knowledge and scope of the Model. Supported Tools are Function and code_execution. Refer to the [Function calling](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/function-calling) and the [Code execution](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/code-execution) guides to learn more.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateContentResponse
member.summary: Response message for [PredictionService.GenerateContent].
Response from the model supporting multiple candidates. Ref: https://ai.google.dev/api/rest/v1beta/GenerateContentResponse

Response from the model supporting multiple candidate responses. Safety ratings and content filtering are reported for both prompt in GenerateContentResponse.prompt_feedback and for each candidate in finish_reason and in safety_ratings. The API: - Returns either all requested candidates or none of them - Returns no candidates at all only if there was something wrong with the prompt (check prompt_feedback) - Reports feedback on each candidate in finish_reason and safety_ratings.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata.PromptTokensDetails

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateMessageRequest
member.summary: Generates a response from the model given an input MessagePrompt.
Request to generate a message response from the model.

member.name: M:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateMessageRequest.#ctor
member.summary: Default constructor.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateMessageRequest.CandidateCount
member.summary: Optional. The number of generated response messages to return. This value must be between [1, 8], inclusive. If unset, this will default to 1.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateMessageRequest.Prompt
member.summary: Required. The structured textual input given to the model as a prompt. Given a prompt, the model will return what it predicts is the next message in the discussion.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateMessageRequest.Temperature
member.summary: Optional. Controls the randomness of the output. Values can range over [0.0,1.0], inclusive. A value closer to 1.0 will produce responses that are more varied, while a value closer to 0.0 will typically result in less surprising responses from the model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateMessageRequest.TopK
member.summary: Optional. The maximum number of tokens to consider when sampling. The model uses combined Top-k and nucleus sampling. Top-k sampling considers the set of top_k most probable tokens.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.GenerateMessageRequest.TopP
member.summary: Optional. The maximum cumulative probability of tokens to consider when sampling. The model uses combined Top-k and nucleus sampling. Nucleus sampling considers the smallest set of tokens whose probability sum is at least top_p.
