member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Permission
member.summary: Permission resource grants user, group or the rest of the world access to the PaLM API resource (e.g. a tuned model, corpus). A role is a collection of permitted operations that allows users to perform specific actions on PaLM API resources. To make them available to users, groups, or service accounts, you assign roles. When you assign a role, you grant permissions that the role contains. There are three concentric roles. Each role is a superset of the previous role's permitted operations: - reader can use the resource (e.g. tuned model, corpus) for inference - writer has reader's permissions and additionally can edit and share - owner has writer's permissions and additionally can delete

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Permission.EmailAddress
member.summary: Optional. Immutable. The email address of the user of group which this permission refers. Field is not set when permission's grantee type is EVERYONE.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Permission.GranteeType
member.summary: Optional. Immutable. The type of the grantee.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Permission.Name
member.summary: Output only. Identifier. The permission name. A unique name will be generated on create. Examples: tunedModels/{tuned_model}/permissions/{permission} corpora/{corpus}/permissions/{permission} Output only.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Permission.Role
member.summary: Required. The role granted by this permission.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Permission.PermissionGranteeType.GranteeTypeUnspecified
member.summary: The default value. This value is unused.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Permission.PermissionGranteeType.User
member.summary: Represents a user. When set, you must provide email_address for the user.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Permission.PermissionGranteeType.Group
member.summary: Represents a group. When set, you must provide email_address for the group.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Permission.PermissionGranteeType.Everyone
member.summary: Represents access to everyone. No extra information is required.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PermissionRole.RoleUnspecified
member.summary: The default value. This value is unused.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PermissionRole.Owner
member.summary: Owner can use, update, share and delete the resource.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PermissionRole.Writer
member.summary: Writer can use, update and share the resource.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PermissionRole.Reader
member.summary: Reader can use the resource.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PlaceAnswerSources
member.summary: Collection of sources that provide answers about the features of a given place in Google Maps. Each PlaceAnswerSources message corresponds to a specific place in Google Maps. The Google Maps tool used these sources in order to answer questions about features of the place (e.g: "does Bar Foo have Wifi" or "is Foo Bar wheelchair accessible?"). Currently we only support review snippets as sources.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PlaceAnswerSources.ReviewSnippets
member.summary: Snippets of reviews that are used to generate answers about the features of a given place in Google Maps.

When this config is set, the default output is replaced with either: - The raw output string. - A parsed output based on a user-defined schema. member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PredictSchemata
member.summary: Contains the schemata used in Model's predictions and explanations via PredictionService.Predict, PredictionService.Explain and BatchPredictionJob.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PredictSchemata.InstanceSchemaUri
member.summary: Immutable. Points to a YAML file stored on Google Cloud Storage describing the format of a single instance, which are used in PredictRequest.instances, ExplainRequest.instances and BatchPredictionJob.input_config. The schema is defined as an OpenAPI 3.0.2 [Schema Object](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/main/versions/3.0.2.md#schemaObject). AutoML Models always have this field populated by Vertex AI. Note: The URI given on output will be immutable and probably different, including the URI scheme, than the one given on input. The output URI will point to a location where the user only has a read access.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PredictSchemata.ParametersSchemaUri
member.summary: Immutable. Points to a YAML file stored on Google Cloud Storage describing the parameters of prediction and explanation via PredictRequest.parameters, ExplainRequest.parameters and BatchPredictionJob.model_parameters. The schema is defined as an OpenAPI 3.0.2 [Schema Object](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/main/versions/3.0.2.md#schemaObject). AutoML Models always have this field populated by Vertex AI, if no parameters are supported, then it is set to an empty string. Note: The URI given on output will be immutable and probably different, including the URI scheme, than the one given on input. The output URI will point to a location where the user only has a read access.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PredictSchemata.PredictionSchemaUri
member.summary: Immutable. Points to a YAML file stored on Google Cloud Storage describing the format of a single prediction produced by this Model, which are returned via PredictResponse.predictions, ExplainResponse.explanations, and BatchPredictionJob.output_config. The schema is defined as an OpenAPI 3.0.2 [Schema Object](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/main/versions/3.0.2.md#schemaObject). AutoML Models always have this field populated by Vertex AI. Note: The URI given on output will be immutable and probably different, including the URI scheme, than the one given on input. The output URI will point to a location where the user only has a read access.

member.summary: Tuning Spec for Preference Optimization.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PreferenceOptimizationSpec.ExportLastCheckpointOnly
member.summary: Optional. If set to true, disable intermediate checkpoints for Preference Optimization and only the last checkpoint will be exported. Otherwise, enable intermediate checkpoints for Preference Optimization. Default is false.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PreferenceOptimizationSpec.HyperParameters
member.summary: Optional. Hyperparameters for Preference Optimization.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PreferenceOptimizationSpec.TrainingDatasetUri
member.summary: Required. Cloud Storage path to file containing training dataset for preference optimization tuning. The dataset must be formatted as a JSONL file.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PreferenceOptimizationSpec.ValidationDatasetUri
member.summary: Optional. Cloud Storage path to file containing validation dataset for preference optimization tuning. The dataset must be formatted as a JSONL file.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Presets
member.summary: Preset configuration for example-based explanations

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Presets.Modality
member.summary: The modality of the uploaded model, which automatically configures the distance measurement and feature normalization for the underlying example index and queries. If your model does not precisely fit one of these types, it is okay to choose the closest type.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Presets.Query
member.summary: Preset option controlling parameters for speed-precision trade-off when querying for examples. If omitted, defaults to PRECISE.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Presets.ModalityType.ModalityUnspecified
member.summary: Should not be set. Added as a recommended best practice for enums

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Presets.ModalityType.Image
member.summary: IMAGE modality

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Presets.ModalityType.Text
member.summary: TEXT modality

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Presets.ModalityType.Tabular
member.summary: TABULAR modality

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Presets.QueryType.Precise
member.summary: More precise neighbors as a trade-off against slower response.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Presets.QueryType.Fast
member.summary: Faster response as a trade-off against less precise neighbors.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PreTunedModel
member.summary: A pre-tuned model for continuous tuning.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PreTunedModel.BaseModel
member.summary: Output only. The name of the base model this PreTunedModel was tuned from.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PreTunedModel.CheckpointId
member.summary: Optional. The source checkpoint id. If not specified, the default checkpoint will be used.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.PreTunedModel.TunedModelName
member.summary: The resource name of the Model. E.g., a model resource name with a specified version id or alias: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@{version_id} projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@{alias} Or, omit the version id to use the default version: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProactivityConfig
member.summary: Config for proactivity features.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProactivityConfig.ProactiveAudio
member.summary: Optional. If enabled, the model can reject responding to the last prompt. For example, this allows the model to ignore out of context speech or to stay silent if the user did not make a request, yet.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe
member.summary: Probe describes a health check to be performed against a container to determine whether it is alive or ready to receive traffic.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe.Exec
member.summary: ExecAction probes the health of a container by executing a command.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe.FailureThreshold
member.summary: Number of consecutive failures before the probe is considered failed. Defaults to 3. Minimum value is 1. Maps to Kubernetes probe argument 'failureThreshold'.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe.Grpc
member.summary: GrpcAction probes the health of a container by sending a gRPC request.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe.HttpGet
member.summary: HttpGetAction probes the health of a container by sending an HTTP GET request.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe.InitialDelaySeconds
member.summary: Number of seconds to wait before starting the probe. Defaults to 0. Minimum value is 0. Maps to Kubernetes probe argument 'initialDelaySeconds'.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe.PeriodSeconds
member.summary: How often (in seconds) to perform the probe. Default to 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1. Must be less than timeout_seconds. Maps to Kubernetes probe argument 'periodSeconds'.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe.SuccessThreshold
member.summary: Number of consecutive successes before the probe is considered successful. Defaults to 1. Minimum value is 1. Maps to Kubernetes probe argument 'successThreshold'.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe.TcpSocket
member.summary: TcpSocketAction probes the health of a container by opening a TCP socket connection.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Probe.TimeoutSeconds
member.summary: Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Defaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1. Must be greater or equal to period_seconds. Maps to Kubernetes probe argument 'timeoutSeconds'.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeExecAction
member.summary: ExecAction specifies a command to execute.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeExecAction.Command
member.summary: Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the command is root ('/') in the container's filesystem. The command is simply exec'd, it is not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions ('|', etc) won't work. To use a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell. Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeGrpcAction
member.summary: GrpcAction checks the health of a container using a gRPC service.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeGrpcAction.Port
member.summary: Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeGrpcAction.Service
member.summary: Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest. See https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md. If this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeHttpGetAction
member.summary: HttpGetAction describes an action based on HTTP Get requests.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeHttpGetAction.Host
member.summary: Host name to connect to, defaults to the model serving container's IP. You probably want to set "Host" in httpHeaders instead.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeHttpGetAction.HttpHeaders
member.summary: Custom headers to set in the request. HTTP allows repeated headers.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeHttpGetAction.Path
member.summary: Path to access on the HTTP server.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeHttpGetAction.Port
member.summary: Number of the port to access on the container. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeHttpGetAction.Scheme
member.summary: Scheme to use for connecting to the host. Defaults to HTTP. Acceptable values are "HTTP" or "HTTPS".

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeHttpHeader
member.summary: HttpHeader describes a custom header to be used in HTTP probes

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeHttpHeader.Name
member.summary: The header field name. This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeHttpHeader.Value
member.summary: The header field value

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeTcpSocketAction
member.summary: TcpSocketAction probes the health of a container by opening a TCP socket connection.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeTcpSocketAction.Host
member.summary: Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the model serving container's IP.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProbeTcpSocketAction.Port
member.summary: Number of the port to access on the container. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ProductImage
member.summary: An image of the product.

A set of the feedback metadata the prompt specified in GenerateContentRequest.content.

member.summary: Output only. A readable block reason message.

member.summary: Configuration for hybrid search.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagEmbeddingModelConfig.VertexPredictionEndpoint
member.summary: The Vertex AI Prediction Endpoint that either refers to a publisher model or an endpoint that is hosting a 1P fine-tuned text embedding model. Endpoints hosting non-1P fine-tuned text embedding models are currently not supported. This is used for dense vector search.

member.summary: Config for hybrid search.

member.summary: Required. The Vertex AI Prediction Endpoint that hosts the embedding model for dense embedding generations.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagEmbeddingModelConfigHybridSearchConfig.SparseEmbeddingConfig
member.summary: Optional. The configuration for sparse embedding generation. This field is optional the default behavior depends on the vector database choice on the RagCorpus.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagEmbeddingModelConfigSparseEmbeddingConfig
member.summary: Configuration for sparse emebdding generation.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagEmbeddingModelConfigSparseEmbeddingConfig.Bm25
member.summary: Use BM25 scoring algorithm.

member.summary: Message for BM25 parameters.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagEmbeddingModelConfigSparseEmbeddingConfigBm25.B
member.summary: Optional. The parameter to control document length normalization. It determines how much the document length affects the final score. b is in the range of [0, 1]. The default value is 0.75.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagEmbeddingModelConfigSparseEmbeddingConfigBm25.K1
member.summary: Optional. The parameter to control term frequency saturation. It determines the scaling between the matching term frequency and final score. k1 is in the range of [1.2, 3]. The default value is 1.2.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagEmbeddingModelConfigSparseEmbeddingConfigBm25.Multilingual
member.summary: Optional. Use multilingual tokenizer if set to true.

member.summary: Required. The endpoint resource name. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/{publisher}/models/{model} or projects/{project}/locations/{location}/endpoints/{endpoint}

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagEmbeddingModelConfigVertexPredictionEndpoint.Model

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagEmbeddingModelConfigVertexPredictionEndpoint.ModelVersionId
member.summary: Output only. Version ID of the model that is deployed on the endpoint. Present only when the endpoint is not a publisher model.

member.summary: The prompt to use for parsing. If not specified, a default prompt will be used.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagFileParsingConfigLlmParser.GlobalMaxParsingRequestsPerMin
member.summary: The maximum number of requests the job is allowed to make to the LLM model per minute in this project. Consult https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/quotas and your document size to set an appropriate value here. If this value is not specified, max_parsing_requests_per_min will be used by indexing pipeline job as the global limit.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagFileParsingConfigLlmParser.MaxParsingRequestsPerMin
member.summary: The maximum number of requests the job is allowed to make to the LLM model per minute. Consult https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/quotas and your document size to set an appropriate value here. If unspecified, a default value of 5000 QPM would be used.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagFileParsingConfigLlmParser.ModelName

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagStore.Name
member.summary: Output only. Immutable. Identifier. The `RagStore` resource name.
member.remarks: It is an ID (name excluding the "ragStores/" prefix) that can contain up to 40 characters that are lowercase alphanumeric or dashes (-). It is output only. The unique name will be derived from `display_name` along with a 12 character random suffix. Example: `ragStores/my-awesome-rag-store-123a456b789c` If `display_name` is not provided, the name will be randomly generated.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagStore.DisplayName
member.summary: Optional. The human-readable display name for the `RagStore`. The display name must be no more than 512 characters in length, including spaces. Example: "Docs on Semantic Retriever"

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagStore.ActiveDocumentsCount
member.summary: Output only. The number of documents in the Ragstore that are active and ready for retrieval.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagStore.FailedDocumentsCount
member.summary: Output only. The number of documents in the Ragstore that have failed processing.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagStore.PendingDocumentsCount
member.summary: Output only. The number of documents in the Ragstore that are being processed.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagStore.SizeBytes
member.summary: Output only. The size in bytes of the Ragstore. This is the total size of all the documents in the Ragstore.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagStore.CreateTime
member.summary: Output only. The Timestamp of when the `RagStore` was created.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagStore.UpdateTime
member.summary: Output only. The Timestamp of when the `RagStore` was last updated.

member.summary: The config for the default RAG-managed Vector DB.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagVectorDbConfigRagManagedDb.Ann
member.summary: Performs an ANN search on RagCorpus. Use this if you have a lot of files (> 10K) in your RagCorpus and want to reduce the search latency.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagVectorDbConfigRagManagedDb.Knn
member.summary: Performs a KNN search on RagCorpus. Default choice if not specified.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagVectorDbConfigRagManagedDbANN
member.summary: Config for ANN search. RagManagedDb uses a tree-based structure to partition data and facilitate faster searches. As a tradeoff, it requires longer indexing time and manual triggering of index rebuild via the ImportRagFiles and UpdateRagCorpus API.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagVectorDbConfigRagManagedDbANN.LeafCount
member.summary: Number of leaf nodes in the tree-based structure. Each leaf node contains groups of closely related vectors along with their corresponding centroid. Recommended value is 10 * sqrt(num of RagFiles in your RagCorpus). Default value is 500.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagVectorDbConfigRagManagedDbANN.TreeDepth
member.summary: The depth of the tree-based structure. Only depth values of 2 and 3 are supported. Recommended value is 2 if you have if you have O(10K) files in the RagCorpus and set this to 3 if more than that. Default value is 2.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RagVectorDbConfigRagManagedDbKNN
member.summary: Config for KNN search.

member.summary: Configures the realtime input behavior in BidiGenerateContent.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RealtimeInputConfig.ActivityHandling
member.summary: Optional. Defines what effect activity has.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RealtimeInputConfig.AutomaticActivityDetection
member.summary: Optional. If not set, automatic activity detection is enabled by default. If automatic voice detection is disabled, the client must send activity signals.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RealtimeInputConfig.TurnCoverage
member.summary: Optional. Defines which input is included in the user's turn.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RealtimeInputConfig.ActivityHandlingType.ActivityHandlingUnspecified
member.summary: If unspecified, the default behavior is `START_OF_ACTIVITY_INTERRUPTS`.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RealtimeInputConfig.ActivityHandlingType.StartOfActivityInterrupts
member.summary: If true, start of activity will interrupt the model's response (also called "barge in"). The model's current response will be cut-off in the moment of the interruption. This is the default behavior.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RealtimeInputConfig.ActivityHandlingType.NoInterruption
member.summary: The model's response will not be interrupted.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RealtimeInputConfig.TurnCoverageType.TurnCoverageUnspecified
member.summary: If unspecified, the default behavior is `TURN_INCLUDES_ONLY_ACTIVITY`.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RealtimeInputConfig.TurnCoverageType.TurnIncludesOnlyActivity
member.summary: The users turn only includes activity since the last turn, excluding inactivity (e.g. silence on the audio stream). This is the default behavior.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RealtimeInputConfig.TurnCoverageType.TurnIncludesAllInput
member.summary: The users turn includes all realtime input since the last turn, including inactivity (e.g. silence on the audio stream).

member.summary: Gets or sets the timeout for this specific request. If set to a positive value, the request will be cancelled if it exceeds this duration. This is achieved by linking a CancellationToken to the request. A value of TimeSpan.Zero (the default) or a negative value means no per-request timeout will be applied beyond any default client timeout.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RetrievalMetadata.GoogleSearchDynamicRetrievalScore
member.summary: Optional. Score indicating how likely information from google search could help answer the prompt. The score is in the range [0, 1], where 0 is the least likely and 1 is the most likely. This score is only populated when google search grounding and dynamic retrieval is enabled. It will be compared to the threshold to determine whether to trigger google search.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyFeedback
member.summary: Safety feedback for an entire request. This field is populated if content in the input and/or response is blocked due to safety settings. SafetyFeedback may not exist for every HarmCategory. Each SafetyFeedback will return the safety settings used by the request as well as the lowest HarmProbability that should be allowed in order to return a result.

member.summary: Safety rating evaluated from content.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyFilterLevel
member.summary: Enum that controls the safety filter level for objectionable content.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyFilterLevel.BlockLowAndAbove
member.summary: Content with NEGLIGIBLE will be allowed.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyFilterLevel.BlockMediumAndAbove
member.summary: Content with NEGLIGIBLE and LOW will be allowed.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyFilterLevel.BlockOnlyHigh
member.summary: Content with NEGLIGIBLE, LOW, and MEDIUM will be allowed.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyFilterLevel.BlockNone
member.summary: All content will be allowed.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating
member.summary: A safety rating for a piece of content. The safety rating contains the harm category and the harm probability level.
A safety rating for a piece of content. The safety rating contains the harm category and the harm probability level. Ref: https://ai.google.dev/api/rest/v1beta/SafetyRating

Safety rating for a piece of content. The safety rating contains the category of harm and the harm probability level in that category for a piece of content. Content is classified for safety across a number of harm categories and the probability of the harm classification is included here.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.OverwrittenThreshold
member.summary: Output only. The overwritten threshold for the safety category of Gemini 2.0 image out. If minors are detected in the output image, the threshold of each safety category will be overwritten if user sets a lower threshold.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.ProbabilityScore
member.summary: Output only. The probability score of harm for this category.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.Severity
member.summary: Output only. The severity of harm for this category.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.SeverityScore
member.summary: Output only. The severity score of harm for this category.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.OverwrittenThresholdType.HarmBlockThresholdUnspecified
member.summary: The harm block threshold is unspecified.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.OverwrittenThresholdType.BlockLowAndAbove
member.summary: Block content with a low harm probability or higher.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.OverwrittenThresholdType.BlockMediumAndAbove
member.summary: Block content with a medium harm probability or higher.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.OverwrittenThresholdType.BlockOnlyHigh
member.summary: Block content with a high harm probability.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.OverwrittenThresholdType.BlockNone
member.summary: Do not block any content, regardless of its harm probability.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.OverwrittenThresholdType.Off
member.summary: Turn off the safety filter entirely.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.Blocked
member.summary: Was this content blocked because of this rating?

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.Category
member.summary: Required. The category for this rating.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetyRating.Probability
member.summary: Required. The probability of harm for this content.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetySetting
member.summary: A safety setting that affects the safety-blocking behavior. A SafetySetting consists of a harm category and a threshold for that category.
A safety setting that affects the safety-blocking behavior. A SafetySetting consists of a harm category and a threshold for that category.
member.remarks: Represents a safety setting that can be used to control the model's behavior. It instructs the model to avoid certain responses given safety measurements based on category. Ref: https://ai.google.dev/api/rest/v1beta/SafetySetting
member.summary: Safety setting, affecting the safety-blocking behavior. Passing a safety setting for a category changes the allowed probability that content is blocked.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetySetting.Method
member.summary: Optional. The method for blocking content. If not specified, the default behavior is to use the probability score.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetySetting.Category
member.summary: Required. The category for this setting.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SafetySetting.Threshold
member.summary: Required. Controls the probability threshold at which harm is blocked.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SampledShapleyAttribution
member.summary: An attribution method that approximates Shapley values for features that contribute to the label being predicted. A sampling strategy is used to approximate the value rather than considering all subsets of features.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SampledShapleyAttribution.PathCount
member.summary: Required. The number of feature permutations to consider when approximating the Shapley values. Valid range of its value is [1, 50], inclusively.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SavedQuery.DisplayName
member.summary: Required. The user-defined name of the SavedQuery. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SavedQuery.Etag
member.summary: Used to perform a consistent read-modify-write update. If not set, a blind "overwrite" update happens.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SavedQuery.Metadata
member.summary: Some additional information about the SavedQuery.

Allowed values: * IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION_SINGLE_LABEL * IMAGE_CLASSIFICATION_MULTI_LABEL * IMAGE_BOUNDING_POLY * IMAGE_BOUNDING_BOX * TEXT_CLASSIFICATION_SINGLE_LABEL * TEXT_CLASSIFICATION_MULTI_LABEL * TEXT_EXTRACTION * TEXT_SENTIMENT * VIDEO_CLASSIFICATION * VIDEO_OBJECT_TRACKING

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling
member.summary: All parameters related to queuing and scheduling of custom jobs.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.DisableRetries
member.summary: Optional. Indicates if the job should retry for internal errors after the job starts running. If true, overrides Scheduling.restart_job_on_worker_restart to false.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.MaxWaitDuration
member.summary: Optional. This is the maximum duration that a job will wait for the requested resources to be provisioned if the scheduling strategy is set to [Strategy.DWS_FLEX_START]. If set to 0, the job will wait indefinitely. The default is 24 hours.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.RestartJobOnWorkerRestart
member.summary: Optional. Restarts the entire CustomJob if a worker gets restarted. This feature can be used by distributed training jobs that are not resilient to workers leaving and joining a job.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.Strategy
member.summary: Optional. This determines which type of scheduling strategy to use.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.Timeout
member.summary: Optional. The maximum job running time. The default is 7 days.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.StrategyType.StrategyUnspecified
member.summary: Strategy will default to STANDARD.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.StrategyType.OnDemand
member.summary: Deprecated. Regular on-demand provisioning strategy.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.StrategyType.LowCost
member.summary: Deprecated. Low cost by making potential use of spot resources.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.StrategyType.Standard
member.summary: Standard provisioning strategy uses regular on-demand resources.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.StrategyType.Spot
member.summary: Spot provisioning strategy uses spot resources.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Scheduling.StrategyType.FlexStart
member.summary: Flex Start strategy uses DWS to queue for resources.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SchedulingType
member.summary: Specifies how the response should be scheduled in the conversation.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SchedulingType.SchedulingUnspecified
member.summary: This value is unused.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SchedulingType.Silent
member.summary: Only add the result to the conversation context, do not interrupt or trigger generation.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SchedulingType.When_Idle
member.summary: Add the result to the conversation context, and prompt to generate output without interrupting ongoing generation.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SchedulingType.Interrupt
member.summary: Add the result to the conversation context, interrupt ongoing generation and prompt to generate output.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema
member.summary: Defines the schema of input and output data. This is a subset of the [OpenAPI 3.0 Schema Object](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#schema-object).

The Schema object allows the definition of input and output data types. These types can be objects, but also primitives and arrays. Represents a select subset of an [OpenAPI 3.0 schema object](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.3#schema).

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.AdditionalProperties
member.summary: Optional. If type is OBJECT, specifies how to handle properties not defined in properties. If it is a boolean false, no additional properties are allowed. If it is a schema, additional properties are allowed if they conform to the schema.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.Defs
member.summary: Optional. defs provides a map of schema definitions that can be reused by ref elsewhere in the schema. Only allowed at root level of the schema.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.Ref
member.summary: Optional. Allows referencing another schema definition to use in place of this schema. The value must be a valid reference to a schema in defs. For example, the following schema defines a reference to a schema node named "Pet": type: object properties: pet: ref: #/defs/Pet defs: Pet: type: object properties: name: type: string The value of the "pet" property is a reference to the schema node named "Pet". See details in https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/structuring

member.name: M:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.FromJsonElement(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Converts a JsonElement representing a JSON Schema to a Schema object.
member.param: The JsonElement containing the JSON Schema
member.returns: A Schema object representing the JSON Schema

member.name: M:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.FromString(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a Schema from a string. Supports either: - A JSON object string representing a JSON Schema, or - A simple OpenAPI type name (string, number, integer, boolean, array, object, null). If the string is a JSON string containing a nested JSON object, it will be parsed recursively.
member.param: JSON schema text or a simple type name
member.exception: Thrown when value is null
Thrown when value is whitespace-only or cannot be parsed into a Schema

member.name: M:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.FromObject(System.Object)
member.summary: Builds a Schema from a .NET object using Json.Schema generation, then maps it into the internal Schema model.

member.summary: Builds a Schema from a .NET type using Json.Schema generation, then maps it into the internal Schema model.

The to use.

member.summary: Builds a parameters from a delegate's signature. Skips framework parameters such as that should not be exposed to the model.

member.returns: A representing the parameters of the delegate as an object with named properties, or null if there are no user-exposed parameters.

member.summary: Builds a response from a delegate's return type. Returns null for void, Task, or ValueTask without a result.
member.param: The delegate whose return type will be used to generate the schema.
member.returns: A representing the return type of the delegate, or null if the return type is void, Task, or ValueTask without a result.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.AnyOf
member.summary: Optional. The value should be validated against any (one or more) of the subschemas in the list.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.Default
member.summary: Optional. Default value of the field. Per JSON Schema, this field is intended for documentation generators and doesn't affect validation. Thus it's included here and ignored so that developers who send schemas with a default field don't get unknown-field errors.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.Description
member.summary: Optional. A brief description of the parameter. This could contain examples of use. Parameter description may be formatted as Markdown.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.Enum
member.summary: Optional. Possible values of the element of Type.STRING with enum format. For example we can define an Enum Direction as : {type:STRING, format:enum, enum:["EAST", NORTH", "SOUTH", "WEST"]}

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Schema.Format

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SlidingWindow
member.summary: The SlidingWindow method operates by discarding content at the beginning of the context window. The resulting context will always begin at the start of a USER role turn. System instructions and any BidiGenerateContentSetup.prefix_turns will always remain at the beginning of the result.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SlidingWindow.TargetTokens
member.summary: The target number of tokens to keep. The default value is trigger_tokens/2. Discarding parts of the context window causes a temporary latency increase so this value should be calibrated to avoid frequent compression operations.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SmoothGradConfig
member.summary: Config for SmoothGrad approximation of gradients. When enabled, the gradients are approximated by averaging the gradients from noisy samples in the vicinity of the inputs. Adding noise can help improve the computed gradients. Refer to this paper for more details: https://arxiv.org/pdf/1706.03825.pdf

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SmoothGradConfig.FeatureNoiseSigma
member.summary: This is similar to noise_sigma, but provides additional flexibility. A separate noise sigma can be provided for each feature, which is useful if their distributions are different. No noise is added to features that are not set. If this field is unset, noise_sigma will be used for all features.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SmoothGradConfig.NoiseSigma
member.summary: This is a single float value and will be used to add noise to all the features. Use this field when all features are normalized to have the same distribution: scale to range [0, 1], [-1, 1] or z-scoring, where features are normalized to have 0-mean and 1-variance. Learn more about [normalization](https://developers.google.com/machine-learning/data-prep/transform/normalization). For best results the recommended value is about 10% - 20% of the standard deviation of the input feature. Refer to section 3.2 of the SmoothGrad paper: https://arxiv.org/pdf/1706.03825.pdf. Defaults to 0.1. If the distribution is different per feature, set feature_noise_sigma instead for each feature.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SmoothGradConfig.NoisySampleCount
member.summary: The number of gradient samples to use for approximation. The higher this number, the more accurate the gradient is, but the runtime complexity increases by this factor as well. Valid range of its value is [1, 50]. Defaults to 3.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Source.SourceUnspecified
member.summary: Used if source is not specified.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Source.Uploaded
member.summary: Indicates the file is uploaded by the user.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Source.Generated
member.summary: Indicates the file is generated by Google.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Source.Registered
member.summary: Indicates the file is a registered, i.e. a Google Cloud Storage file.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SpeechConfig.LanguageCode
member.summary: Optional. The IETF [BCP-47](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/bcp/bcp47.txt) language code that the user configured the app to use. Used for speech recognition and synthesis. Valid values are: de-DE, en-AU, en-GB, en-IN, en-US, es-US, fr-FR, hi-IN, pt-BR, ar-XA, es-ES, fr-CA, id-ID, it-IT, ja-JP, tr-TR, vi-VN, bn-IN, gu-IN, kn-IN, ml-IN, mr-IN, ta-IN, te-IN, nl-NL, ko-KR, cmn-CN, pl-PL, ru-RU, and th-TH.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SpeechConfig.MultiSpeakerVoiceConfig
member.summary: Optional. The configuration for the multi-speaker setup. It is mutually exclusive with the voice_config field.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.SpeechConfig.VoiceConfig
member.summary: The configuration in case of single-voice output.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Stable
member.summary: State when the featureOnlineStore configuration is not being updated and the fields reflect the current configuration of the featureOnlineStore. The featureOnlineStore is usable in this state.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Updating
member.summary: The state of the featureOnlineStore configuration when it is being updated. During an update, the fields reflect either the original configuration or the updated configuration of the featureOnlineStore. The featureOnlineStore is still usable in this state.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Default
member.summary: The default behavior of whether to enable the monitoring. EntityType-level config: disabled. Feature-level config: inherited from the configuration of EntityType this Feature belongs to.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Enabled
member.summary: Explicitly enables import features analysis. EntityType-level config: by default enables import features analysis for all Features under it. Feature-level config: enables import features analysis regardless of the EntityType-level config.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Disabled
member.summary: Explicitly disables import features analysis. EntityType-level config: by default disables import features analysis for all Features under it. Feature-level config: disables import features analysis regardless of the EntityType-level config.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Requested
member.summary: Indicates that a specific NasTrial has been requested, but it has not yet been suggested by the service.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Stopping
member.summary: Indicates that the NasTrial should stop according to the service.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Infeasible
member.summary: Indicates that the NasTrial should not be attempted again. The service will set a NasTrial to INFEASIBLE when it's done but missing the final_measurement.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Paused
member.summary: The schedule is paused. No new runs will be created until the schedule is resumed. Already started runs will be allowed to complete.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Completed
member.summary: The Schedule is completed. No new runs will be scheduled. Already started runs will be allowed to complete. Schedules in completed state cannot be paused or resumed.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.State.Inactive
member.summary: The study is stopped due to an internal error.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Status
member.summary: The Status type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each Status message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

The type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Status.Code
member.summary: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Status.Details
member.summary: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Status.Message
member.summary: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

member.summary: A list of tokens from the input.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool
member.summary: Tool details that the model may use to generate response. A Tool is a piece of code that enables the system to interact with external systems to perform an action, or set of actions, outside of knowledge and scope of the model. A Tool object should contain exactly one type of Tool (e.g FunctionDeclaration, Retrieval or GoogleSearchRetrieval).

Tool details that the model may use to generate response. A Tool is a piece of code that enables the system to interact with external systems to perform an action, or set of actions, outside of knowledge and scope of the model. Next ID: 15

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.EnterpriseWebSearch
member.summary: Optional. Tool to support searching public web data, powered by Vertex AI Search and Sec4 compliance.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.ParallelAiSearch
member.summary: Optional. If specified, Vertex AI will use Parallel.ai to search for information to answer user queries. The search results will be grounded on Parallel.ai and presented to the model for response generation

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.Retrieval
member.summary: Optional. Retrieval tool type. System will always execute the provided retrieval tool(s) to get external knowledge to answer the prompt. Retrieval results are presented to the model for generation.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.CodeExecution
member.summary: Optional. Enables the model to execute code as part of generation.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.ComputerUse
member.summary: Optional. Tool to support the model interacting directly with the computer. If enabled, it automatically populates computer-use specific Function Declarations.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.FileSearch
member.summary: Optional. FileSearch tool type. Tool to retrieve knowledge from Semantic Retrieval corpora.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.FunctionDeclarations
member.summary: Optional. A list of FunctionDeclarations available to the model that can be used for function calling. The model or system does not execute the function. Instead the defined function may be returned as a FunctionCall with arguments to the client side for execution. The model may decide to call a subset of these functions by populating FunctionCall in the response. The next conversation turn may contain a FunctionResponse with the Content.role "function" generation context for the next model turn.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.GoogleMaps
member.summary: Optional. Tool that allows grounding the model's response with geospatial context related to the user's query.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.GoogleSearch
member.summary: Optional. GoogleSearch tool type. Tool to support Google Search in Model. Powered by Google.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.GoogleSearchRetrieval
member.summary: Optional. Retrieval tool that is powered by Google search.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.McpServers
member.summary: Optional. MCP Servers to connect to.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.Tool.UrlContext
member.summary: Optional. Tool to support URL context retrieval.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolCodeExecution
member.summary: Tool that executes code generated by the model, and automatically returns the result to the model. See also [ExecutableCode]and [CodeExecutionResult] which are input and output to this tool.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolComputerUse
member.summary: Tool to support computer use.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolComputerUse.Environment
member.summary: Required. The environment being operated.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolComputerUse.ExcludedPredefinedFunctions
member.summary: Optional. By default, [predefined functions](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/computer-use#supported-actions) are included in the final model call. Some of them can be explicitly excluded from being automatically included. This can serve two purposes: 1. Using a more restricted / different action space. 2. Improving the definitions / instructions of predefined functions.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolConfig
member.summary: Tool config. This config is shared for all tools provided in the request.
The Tool configuration containing parameters for specifying Tool use in the request.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolConfig.FunctionCallingConfig
member.summary: Optional. Function calling config.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolConfig.RetrievalConfig
member.summary: Optional. Retrieval config.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolGoogleSearch
member.summary: GoogleSearch tool type. Tool to support Google Search in Model. Powered by Google.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolGoogleSearch.BlockingConfidence
member.summary: Optional. Sites with confidence level chosen & above this value will be blocked from the search results.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolGoogleSearch.ExcludeDomains
member.summary: Optional. List of domains to be excluded from the search results. The default limit is 2000 domains. Example: ["amazon.com", "facebook.com"].

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolParallelAiSearch
member.summary: ParallelAiSearch tool type. A tool that uses the Parallel.ai search engine for grounding.
ParallelAiSearch tool type. A tool that uses the Parallel.ai search engine for grounding.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolParallelAiSearch.ApiKey
member.summary: Optional. The API key for ParallelAiSearch. If an API key is not provided, the system will attempt to verify access by checking for an active Parallel.ai subscription through the Google Cloud Marketplace. See https://docs.parallel.ai/search/search-quickstart for more details.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.ToolParallelAiSearch.CustomConfigs
member.summary: Optional. Custom configs for ParallelAiSearch. This field can be used to pass any parameter from the Parallel.ai Search API. See the Parallel.ai documentation for the full list of available parameters and their usage: https://docs.parallel.ai/api-reference/search-beta/search Currently only source_policy, excerpts, max_results, mode, fetch_policy can be set via this field. For example: { "source_policy": { "include_domains": ["google.com", "wikipedia.org"], "exclude_domains": ["example.com"] }, "fetch_policy": { "max_age_seconds": 3600 } }

member.summary: The Model Registry Model and Online Prediction Endpoint associated with this TuningJob.
A fine-tuned model created using ModelService.CreateTunedModel.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.Checkpoints
member.summary: Output only. The checkpoints associated with this TunedModel. This field is only populated for tuning jobs that enable intermediate checkpoints.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.Endpoint
member.summary: Output only. A resource name of an Endpoint. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/endpoints/{endpoint}.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.Model
member.summary: Output only. The resource name of the TunedModel. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@{version_id} When tuning from a base model, the version ID will be 1. For continuous tuning, if the provided tuned_model_display_name is set and different from parent model's display name, the tuned model will have a new parent model with version 1. Otherwise the version id will be incremented by 1 from the last version ID in the parent model. E.g., projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models/{model}@{last_version_id + 1}

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.BaseModel
member.summary: Immutable. The name of the Model to tune. Example: models/gemini-1.5-flash-001

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.CreateTime
member.summary: Output only. The timestamp when this model was created.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.Description
member.summary: Optional. A short description of this model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.DisplayName
member.summary: Optional. The name to display for this model in user interfaces. The display name must be up to 40 characters including spaces.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.Name
member.summary: Output only. The tuned model name. A unique name will be generated on create. Example: tunedModels/az2mb0bpw6i If display_name is set on create, the id portion of the name will be set by concatenating the words of the display_name with hyphens and adding a random portion for uniqueness. Example: * display_name = Sentence Translator * name = tunedModels/sentence-translator-u3b7m

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.ReaderProjectNumbers
member.summary: Optional. List of project numbers that have read access to the tuned model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.State
member.summary: Output only. The state of the tuned model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.Temperature
member.summary: Optional. Controls the randomness of the output. Values can range over [0.0,1.0], inclusive. A value closer to 1.0 will produce responses that are more varied, while a value closer to 0.0 will typically result in less surprising responses from the model. This value specifies default to be the one used by the base model while creating the model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.TopK
member.summary: Optional. For Top-k sampling. Top-k sampling considers the set of top_k most probable tokens. This value specifies default to be used by the backend while making the call to the model. This value specifies default to be the one used by the base model while creating the model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.TopP
member.summary: Optional. For Nucleus sampling. Nucleus sampling considers the smallest set of tokens whose probability sum is at least top_p. This value specifies default to be the one used by the base model while creating the model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.TunedModelSource
member.summary: Optional. TunedModel to use as the starting point for training the new model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.TuningTask
member.summary: Required. The tuning task that creates the tuned model.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.UpdateTime
member.summary: Output only. The timestamp when this model was updated.

member.name: F:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TunedModelResponse.StateType.StateUnspecified
member.summary: The default value. This value is unused.

member.summary: The model is being created.

member.summary: The model is ready to be used.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TuningContent
member.summary: The structured datatype containing multi-part content of an example message. This is a subset of the Content proto used during model inference with limited type support. A `Content` includes a `role` field designating the producer of the `Content` and a `parts` field containing multi-part data that contains the content of the message turn.

The structured datatype containing multi-part content of an example message. This is a subset of the Content proto used during model inference with limited type support. A Content includes a role field designating the producer of the Content and a parts field containing multi-part data that contains the content of the message turn.

member.summary: Represents a TuningJob that runs with Google owned models.

member.summary: The base model that is being tuned. See [Supported models](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/model-reference/tuning#supported_models).

member.summary: Output only. Time when the TuningJob was created.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TuningJob.CustomBaseModel
member.summary: Optional. The user-provided path to custom model weights. Set this field to tune a custom model. The path must be a Cloud Storage directory that contains the model weights in .safetensors format along with associated model metadata files. If this field is set, the base_model field must still be set to indicate which base model the custom model is derived from. This feature is only available for open source models.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TuningJob.Description
member.summary: Optional. The description of the TuningJob.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TuningJob.DistillationSpec
member.summary: Tuning Spec for Distillation.

member.summary: Customer-managed encryption key options for a TuningJob. If this is set, then all resources created by the TuningJob will be encrypted with the provided encryption key.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.TuningJob.EndTime

Label keys and values can be no longer than 64 characters (Unicode codepoints), can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. See https://goo.gl/xmQnxf for more information and examples of labels.

member.summary: A `Web` chunk is a piece of evidence that comes from a web page. It contains the URI of the web page, the title of the page, and the domain of the page. This is used to provide the user with a link to the source of the information.

member.summary: The domain of the web page that contains the evidence. This can be used to filter out low-quality sources. This field is not supported in Gemini API.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.WhiteSpaceConfig
member.summary: Configuration for a white space chunking algorithm [white space delimited].

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.WhiteSpaceConfig.MaxOverlapTokens
member.summary: Maximum number of overlapping tokens between two adjacent chunks.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.WhiteSpaceConfig.MaxTokensPerChunk
member.summary: Maximum number of tokens per chunk. Tokens are defined as words for this chunking algorithm. Note: we are defining tokens as words split by whitespace as opposed to the output of a tokenizer. The context window of the latest gemini embedding model as of 2025-04-17 is currently 8192 tokens. We assume that the average word is 5 characters. Therefore, we set the upper limit to 2**9, which is 512 words, or 2560 tokens, assuming worst case a character per token. This is a conservative estimate meant to prevent context window overflow.

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.XraiAttribution
member.summary: An explanation method that redistributes Integrated Gradients attributions to segmented regions, taking advantage of the model's fully differentiable structure. Refer to this paper for more details: https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.02825 Supported only by image Models.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.XraiAttribution.BlurBaselineConfig
member.summary: Config for XRAI with blur baseline. When enabled, a linear path from the maximally blurred image to the input image is created. Using a blurred baseline instead of zero (black image) is motivated by the BlurIG approach explained here: https://arxiv.org/abs/2004.03383

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.XraiAttribution.SmoothGradConfig
member.summary: Config for SmoothGrad approximation of gradients. When enabled, the gradients are approximated by averaging the gradients from noisy samples in the vicinity of the inputs. Adding noise can help improve the computed gradients. Refer to this paper for more details: https://arxiv.org/pdf/1706.03825.pdf

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.XraiAttribution.StepCount
member.summary: Required. The number of steps for approximating the path integral. A good value to start is 50 and gradually increase until the sum to diff property is met within the desired error range. Valid range of its value is [1, 100], inclusively.

member.name: M:Mscc.GenerativeAI.GenerativeModel.ListTunedModels(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Boolean},Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets a list of available tuned models and their descriptions.
member.returns: List of available tuned models.
member.param: The maximum number of Models to return (per page).
A page token, received from a previous ListModels call. Provide the pageToken returned by one request as an argument to the next request to retrieve the next page.

Optional. A filter is a full text search over the tuned model's description and display name. By default, results will not include tuned models shared with everyone. Additional operators: - owner:me - writers:me - readers:me - readers:everyone

When set to `true`, operations that are reachable are returned as normal, and those that are unreachable are returned in the [ListOperationsResponse.unreachable] field. This can only be `true` when reading across collections e.g. when `parent` is set to `"projects/example/locations/-"`. This field is not by default supported and will result in an `UNIMPLEMENTED` error if set unless explicitly documented otherwise in service or product specific documentation.
Options for the request.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.

member.name: M:Mscc.GenerativeAI.GenerativeModel.ListModels(System.Boolean,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String,System.String,Mscc.GenerativeAI.Types.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Lists the [`Model`s](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models/gemini) available through the Gemini API.
member.returns: List of available models.
member.param: Flag, whether models or tuned models shall be returned.
The maximum number of `Models` to return (per page). If unspecified, 50 models will be returned per page. This method returns at most 1000 models per page, even if you pass a larger page_size.
A page token, received from a previous ListModels call. Provide the pageToken returned by one request as an argument to the next request to retrieve the next page.

Optional. A filter is a full text search over the tuned model's description and display name. By default, results will not include tuned models shared with everyone. Additional operators: - owner:me - writers:me - readers:me - readers:everyone
Options for the request.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.exception: Thrown when the functionality is not supported by the model.
Thrown when the request fails to execute.

GOOGLE_ACCESS_TOKEN Optional. Access token provided by OAuth 2.0 or Application Default Credentials (ADC).

member.name: T:Mscc.GenerativeAI.InteractionsModel
member.summary: The Gemini Interactions API is an experimental API that allows developers to build generative AI applications using Gemini models. Gemini is a highly capable multimodal model that can understand and process various types of information, including language, images, audio, video, and code. The API supports use cases like reasoning across text and images, content generation, dialogue agents, summarization, and classification.

member.summary: The text chunk.

member.name: P:Mscc.GenerativeAI.Context.Score
member.summary: According to the underlying Vector DB and the selected metric type, the score can be either the distance or the similarity between the query and the context and its range depends on the metric type.
member.remarks: For example, if the metric type is COSINE_DISTANCE, it represents the distance between the query and the context. The larger the distance, the less relevant the context is to the query. The range is [0, 2], while 0 means the most relevant and 2 means the least relevant.
